scene6.title: L-102 ARTIFICIAL INTELLIGENCE & MACHINE LEARNING LAB

hotSpots.text: L-102 ARTIFICIAL INTELLIGENCE & MACHINE LEARNING LAB

clickHandlerArgs.title: L-102 ARTIFICIAL INTELLIGENCE & MACHINE LEARNING LAB
clickHandlerArgs.description:
Purpose

  • Designing, training, and deploying Machine Learning and Deep Learning models
  • Exploring Artificial Intelligence techniques including NLP, Computer Vision, and Reinforcement Learning
  • Working with real-world datasets for predictive analytics and pattern recognition
  • Building intelligent systems using supervised, unsupervised, and semi-supervised learning

Key Equipment

  • High-performance GPU workstations (NVIDIA CUDA-enabled) for model training
  • AI/ML frameworks – TensorFlow, PyTorch, Keras, and Scikit-learn
  • Jupyter Notebook and Google Colab environments for interactive development
  • Large-scale datasets and data preprocessing pipelines
  • Cloud-based AI services – AWS SageMaker, Google AI Platform, and Azure ML

Skills Developed

  • Machine Learning model design, training, and evaluation
  • Deep Learning – CNNs, RNNs, Transformers, and GANs
  • Natural Language Processing and Computer Vision applications
  • Data preprocessing, feature engineering, and exploratory data analysis
  • Model deployment, optimization, and MLOps best practices

clickHandlerArgs.title: Apple Authorized Training Center
clickHandlerArgs.description:
Purpose

  • Providing official Apple-certified training for iOS, macOS, watchOS, and visionOS app development
  • Hands-on learning of Swift programming, SwiftUI, and UIKit frameworks
  • Building production-ready applications for the Apple ecosystem
  • Preparing students for Apple Developer certifications and App Store deployment

Key Equipment

  • Apple iMac with M1 Chip and 8GB Memory
  • iPad Pro 6th Generation (12.9-inch)
  • iPad 10th Generation
  • iPhone 14
  • iPhone 14 Pro
  • Xcode IDE with Interface Builder, Instruments, and Simulator
  • Large interactive display for live coding demonstrations and UI previews

Skills Developed

  • Swift programming and modern app architecture (MVVM, MVC)
  • UI development using SwiftUI and UIKit with responsive layouts
  • Core Data, CloudKit, and RESTful API integration
  • App Store submission, TestFlight beta testing, and CI/CD workflows
  • AR development with ARKit and cross-platform Apple ecosystem integration

clickHandlerArgs.title: iPhone 14 Pro
clickHandlerArgs.description:
iPhone 14 Pro

  • Display: 6.1-inch Super Retina XDR with ProMotion (120Hz) and Always-On display
  • Chip: A16 Bionic chip with 6-core CPU and 5-core GPU
  • Camera: 48MP Main + 12MP Ultra Wide + 12MP Telephoto with 3x optical zoom
  • Storage: Available in 128GB, 256GB, 512GB, and 1TB
  • Features: Dynamic Island, Crash Detection, Emergency SOS via satellite, Ceramic Shield front, Surgical-grade stainless steel design
  • Use in Lab: Used for testing and deploying iOS applications built with Swift and SwiftUI, debugging with Xcode Instruments, and real-device performance profiling

clickHandlerArgs.title: iPhone 14
clickHandlerArgs.description:
iPhone 14

  • Display: 6.1-inch Super Retina XDR OLED display
  • Chip: A15 Bionic chip with 5-core GPU
  • Camera: 12MP Main with larger sensor + 12MP Ultra Wide, Photonic Engine
  • Storage: Available in 128GB, 256GB, and 512GB
  • Features: Crash Detection, Emergency SOS via satellite, Action Mode for video stabilization, Ceramic Shield front, aerospace-grade aluminum design
  • Use in Lab: Used for hands-on iOS app development and testing, SwiftUI previews on real hardware, and learning Apple's Human Interface Guidelines through real-device interaction

clickHandlerArgs.title: iMac with M1 Chip
clickHandlerArgs.description:
iMac with M1 Chip

  • Display: 24-inch 4.5K Retina display with P3 wide color and 500 nits brightness
  • Chip: Apple M1 chip with 8-core CPU and 7-core or 8-core GPU
  • Memory: 8GB unified memory (configurable up to 16GB)
  • Storage: 256GB or 512GB SSD (configurable up to 1TB)
  • Features: 1080p FaceTime HD camera, six-speaker sound system with Spatial Audio, Magic Keyboard with Touch ID, two Thunderbolt/USB 4 ports
  • Use in Lab: Primary development workstation for building iOS, macOS, watchOS, and visionOS applications using Xcode IDE, running simulators, and performing UI/UX design with SwiftUI previews

clickHandlerArgs.title: System Support Cell
clickHandlerArgs.description:
System Support Cell

  • Type: Centralized technical support hub for IT infrastructure and user assistance
  • Services: Hardware troubleshooting, software installation and configuration, network connectivity support, printer and peripheral setup, user account management
  • Equipment: Diagnostic tools, spare computer parts, network testing equipment, software deployment tools
  • Support Channels: Walk-in support, helpdesk ticketing system, remote assistance tools, phone and email support
  • Service Level: Tier-1 and Tier-2 support for faculty, staff, and students, incident tracking and resolution, SLA management
  • Use in Lab: Demonstrates IT service management best practices, technical support workflows, and helpdesk operations essential for maintaining campus IT infrastructure

scene10.title: L-102 ARTIFICIAL INTELLIGENCE & MACHINE LEARNING LAB

scene11.title: L-102 ARTIFICIAL INTELLIGENCE & MACHINE LEARNING LAB

scene12.title: L-102 ARTIFICIAL INTELLIGENCE & MACHINE LEARNING LAB

scene19.title: L-103 CLOUD COMPUTING & L-104 CYBAR SECURITY LAB

clickHandlerArgs.title: L-104 Cyber Security Lab
clickHandlerArgs.description:
Purpose

  • Monitoring client's critical data and network activities
  • Detecting and responding to cybersecurity threats
  • Learning real-time security monitoring and incident response
  • Improving data protection and threat prevention techniques

Key Equipment

  • SIEM tools (Security Information and Event Management)
  • Firewalls and Intrusion Detection Systems (IDS/IPS)
  • Security monitoring dashboards
  • Threat intelligence and analysis tools
  • Secure servers and network monitoring systems

Skills Developed

  • Security threat detection and analysis
  • Incident response and mitigation
  • Network security monitoring
  • Cyber attack investigation
  • Data protection and risk management

clickHandlerArgs.title: L-103 Cloud Computing Lab
clickHandlerArgs.description:
Purpose

  • Deploying and managing cloud-based infrastructure and services
  • Working with virtualization, containerization, and cloud orchestration
  • Learning IaaS, PaaS, and SaaS cloud service models
  • Building scalable and distributed applications on cloud platforms

Key Equipment

  • Cloud platforms – AWS, Microsoft Azure, and Google Cloud (GCP)
  • Virtualization tools – VMware, VirtualBox, and Hyper-V
  • Containerization – Docker and Kubernetes clusters
  • High-performance servers with multi-core processors and high RAM
  • Networking equipment for cloud simulation and SDN labs

Skills Developed

  • Cloud architecture design and deployment
  • DevOps practices – CI/CD pipelines, Infrastructure as Code
  • Containerization and microservices orchestration
  • Cloud security, identity management, and access control
  • Serverless computing and auto-scaling configurations

clickHandlerArgs.title: MacBook Pro
clickHandlerArgs.description:
MacBook Pro

  • Display: 13.3-inch or 14-inch Liquid Retina XDR display with ProMotion (up to 120Hz)
  • Chip: Apple M1 / M1 Pro / M2 chip with high-performance CPU and GPU cores
  • Memory: 8GB to 32GB unified memory for seamless multitasking
  • Storage: 256GB to 2TB SSD for fast read/write speeds
  • Features: MagSafe charging, Thunderbolt ports, Magic Keyboard with Touch ID, studio-quality three-mic array, up to 20 hours battery life
  • Use in Lab: Portable development machine for building and testing Apple ecosystem apps, on-the-go coding with Xcode, Swift Playgrounds, and running macOS/iOS simulators

clickHandlerArgs.title: iPad Pro 6th Generation (12.9-inch)
clickHandlerArgs.description:
iPad Pro 6th Generation (12.9-inch)

  • Display: 12.9-inch Liquid Retina XDR display with ProMotion (120Hz), P3 wide color, and True Tone
  • Chip: Apple M2 chip with 8-core CPU and 10-core GPU
  • Memory: 8GB or 16GB unified memory
  • Storage: Available in 128GB, 256GB, 512GB, 1TB, and 2TB
  • Features: Face ID, LiDAR Scanner, 12MP Wide + 10MP Ultra Wide rear cameras, Thunderbolt/USB 4 port, Apple Pencil (2nd gen) support, Magic Keyboard compatible
  • Use in Lab: Used for Swift Playgrounds development, ARKit augmented reality prototyping with LiDAR, iPadOS app testing, and interactive UI design with Apple Pencil

clickHandlerArgs.title: iPad 10th Generation
clickHandlerArgs.description:
iPad 10th Generation

  • Display: 10.9-inch Liquid Retina display with True Tone and 500 nits brightness
  • Chip: A14 Bionic chip with 6-core CPU and 4-core GPU
  • Storage: Available in 64GB and 256GB
  • Features: Touch ID on top button, 12MP rear camera, 12MP Ultra Wide front camera with Center Stage, USB-C connector, Wi-Fi 6, Apple Pencil (1st gen with USB-C adapter) support
  • Use in Lab: Entry-level device for learning iPadOS app development, testing responsive UI layouts, Swift Playgrounds coding, and exploring basic AR experiences with ARKit

clickHandlerArgs.title: L-108 Network Architecture Lab
clickHandlerArgs.description:
Purpose

  • Hands-on learning in network design and configuration
  • Real-world network scenario simulation
  • Network planning, implementation, and optimization

Key Equipment

  • Advanced routers and managed switches
  • Enterprise-grade servers
  • Network analysis and monitoring tools
  • Security testing platforms

Skills Developed

  • Network topology design and implementation
  • Configuration and troubleshooting
  • Network security and analysis
  • IT infrastructure management

scene30.title: L-107 EXTENDED REALITY & GAME DEVELOPMENT LAB

scene31.title: L-107 EXTENDED REALITY & GAME DEVELOPMENT LAB

clickHandlerArgs.title: L-106 IOT Lab
clickHandlerArgs.description: The Internet of Things Lab features Arduino boards, Raspberry Pi devices, sensors, and smart device components for hands-on IoT development and research.

hotSpots.text: L-107 Extended Reality & Game Development Lab

clickHandlerArgs.title: L-107 Extended Reality & Game Development Lab
clickHandlerArgs.description: The Extended Reality & Game Development Lab is a specialized facility designed for hands-on learning and research in Virtual Reality (VR), Augmented Reality (AR), and Mixed Reality (MR). The lab is equipped with advanced XR devices such as Microsoft HoloLens and Meta VR headsets, high-performance systems, and development tools used for creating immersive applications and games.This lab enables students to work on real-time game development, 3D visualization, interactive simulations, and virtual walkthroughs. It supports learning in areas like Unity/Unreal game engines, XR application development, human–computer interaction, and immersive technology research. The lab plays a key role in enhancing practical skills, innovation, and industry-ready experience in extended reality and game development domains.

scene32.title: L-107 EXTENDED REALITY & GAME DEVELOPMENT LAB

clickHandlerArgs.title: VR Headsets
clickHandlerArgs.description: Meta Quest headsets are fully immersive virtual reality (VR) devices designed for gaming, development, and training applications. They provide high-resolution displays, motion-tracked controllers, and spatial audio to create realistic virtual environments. These headsets are commonly used for VR game development, virtual simulations, interactive learning, and XR research. Meta Quest devices support standalone operation as well as PC-based development, making them ideal for testing and building immersive VR experiences.

clickHandlerArgs.title: Hololens
clickHandlerArgs.description: Microsoft HoloLens is a mixed reality (MR) headset that blends digital holograms with the real world. Unlike traditional VR, it allows users to see and interact with virtual 3D objects while remaining aware of their physical surroundings. HoloLens is widely used in education, research, engineering, healthcare, and industrial training for tasks such as 3D visualization, simulation, remote assistance, and collaborative development. It enables hands-free interaction using gestures, voice commands, and eye tracking.

clickHandlerArgs.title: Holoboard
clickHandlerArgs.description: HoloBoard is a next-generation augmented reality (AR) wearable device designed to seamlessly blend digital content with the real world. It features a sleek, futuristic headset with a transparent visor that projects high-quality holographic visuals directly into the user’s field of view.🔹 Key Features  Immersive AR Experience: Displays 3D holograms, interactive interfaces, and real-time data overlays. Lightweight & Ergonomic Design: Built for comfort during extended use, with an adjustable headband. Gesture & Controller Support: Can be operated using hand gestures or a compact handheld controller. High-Resolution Optics: Ensures clear and sharp visuals with minimal distortion. Real-Time Interaction: Allows users to interact with virtual objects as if they exist in the physical environment.🔹 Applications Education & Training: Visualize complex concepts in 3D for better understanding. Gaming & Entertainment: Experience immersive gameplay and interactive media. Business & Collaboration: Conduct virtual meetings with holographic presentations. Healthcare & Engineering: Assist in simulations, diagnostics, and design visualization.

clickHandlerArgs.title: player
clickHandlerArgs.description: HoloBoard is a next-generation augmented reality (AR) wearable device designed to seamlessly blend digital content with the real world. It features a sleek, futuristic headset with a transparent visor that projects high-quality holographic visuals directly into the user’s field of view.🔹 Key Features  Immersive AR Experience: Displays 3D holograms, interactive interfaces, and real-time data overlays. Lightweight & Ergonomic Design: Built for comfort during extended use, with an adjustable headband. Gesture & Controller Support: Can be operated using hand gestures or a compact handheld controller. High-Resolution Optics: Ensures clear and sharp visuals with minimal distortion. Real-Time Interaction: Allows users to interact with virtual objects as if they exist in the physical environment.🔹 Applications Education & Training: Visualize complex concepts in 3D for better understanding. Gaming & Entertainment: Experience immersive gameplay and interactive media. Business & Collaboration: Conduct virtual meetings with holographic presentations. Healthcare & Engineering: Assist in simulations, diagnostics, and design visualization.

scene33.title: L-107 EXTENDED REALITY & GAME DEVELOPMENT LAB

scene34.title: L-107 EXTENDED REALITY & GAME DEVELOPMENT LAB

scene35.title: L-106 INTERNET OF THINGS INNOVATION LAB

scene36.title: L-106 INTERNET OF THINGS INNOVATION LAB

clickHandlerArgs.title: ARM7 LPC2148 Development Board
clickHandlerArgs.description:
ARM7 LPC2148 Development Board

  • Processor: NXP LPC2148 – 32-bit ARM7TDMI-S microcontroller running at up to 60 MHz
  • Memory: 512KB on-chip Flash, 32KB+8KB SRAM
  • Peripherals: Two 10-bit ADCs, one 10-bit DAC, two UARTs, SPI, I2C, USB 2.0 Full Speed, PWM, and real-time clock
  • GPIO: Up to 45 general-purpose I/O pins with configurable pull-up/pull-down
  • Features: In-System Programming (ISP) and In-Application Programming (IAP), JTAG debugging support, Vectored Interrupt Controller (VIC), supports embedded C and assembly programming
  • Use in Lab: Used for learning embedded C programming, real-time operating systems (RTOS), interfacing sensors and actuators, and building IoT edge devices with ARM architecture

clickHandlerArgs.title: Raspberry Pi
clickHandlerArgs.description:
Raspberry Pi

  • Type: Single-board computer (SBC)
  • Processor: Quad-core ARM Cortex-A72 (64-bit) @ 1.5GHz
  • Memory: 4GB LPDDR4 SDRAM
  • Storage: MicroSD card slot (up to 256GB)
  • Connectivity: Gigabit Ethernet, dual-band 802.11ac Wi-Fi, Bluetooth 5.0, 2× USB 3.0, 2× USB 2.0
  • Features: 40-pin GPIO header, dual micro-HDMI ports (up to 4K resolution), CSI camera port, DSI display port, hardware video decoding
  • Use in Lab: Used as a low-cost IoT gateway, edge computing platform, machine learning inference engine, and for building custom embedded systems and robotics projects

clickHandlerArgs.title: ATmega328 Development Board
clickHandlerArgs.description:
ATmega328 Development Board

  • Processor: Atmel ATmega328P – 8-bit AVR RISC-based microcontroller @ 16 MHz
  • Memory: 32KB Flash, 2KB SRAM, 1KB EEPROM
  • I/O Pins: 23 programmable I/O lines with 6 ADC channels (10-bit resolution)
  • Communication: USART, SPI, and I2C (TWI) serial interfaces
  • Features: Low-power modes, watchdog timer, on-chip analog comparator, external/internal interrupts, PWM output, Arduino IDE compatible
  • Use in Lab: Core microcontroller for learning embedded programming, sensor interfacing, timer/counter operations, and serial communication protocols in IoT applications

clickHandlerArgs.title: Arduino Uno
clickHandlerArgs.description:
Arduino Uno

  • Microcontroller: ATmega328P @ 16 MHz clock speed
  • Operating Voltage: 5V (input 7–12V recommended)
  • I/O Pins: 14 digital I/O pins (6 PWM), 6 analog input pins
  • Memory: 32KB Flash, 2KB SRAM, 1KB EEPROM
  • Communication: UART, SPI, I2C; USB Type-B for programming
  • Features: Open-source platform, extensive library support, compatible with hundreds of shields and sensors, Arduino IDE for easy programming
  • Use in Lab: Entry-level board for IoT projects – reading sensor data, controlling actuators, building smart systems, and prototyping connected devices with Wi-Fi/Bluetooth modules

clickHandlerArgs.title: Ultrasonic Distance Sensor (HC-SR04)
clickHandlerArgs.description:
Ultrasonic Distance Sensor (HC-SR04)

  • Type: Non-contact distance measurement sensor using ultrasonic sound waves
  • Range: 2 cm to 400 cm with accuracy of ±3 mm
  • Operating Voltage: 5V DC, 15 mA operating current
  • Frequency: 40 kHz ultrasonic transducer
  • Pins: VCC, Trigger, Echo, GND – easy 4-pin interface
  • Features: Measures distance by sending a trigger pulse and timing the echo return; works with Arduino, Raspberry Pi, ESP32 platforms
  • Use in Lab: Used in obstacle detection, parking assist systems, liquid level monitoring, and robotics navigation projects

clickHandlerArgs.title: Buzzer
clickHandlerArgs.description:
Buzzer

  • Type: Active/Passive piezoelectric buzzer with built-in driver circuit
  • Operating Voltage: 3.3V to 5V DC
  • Sound Output: 85 dB at 10 cm distance, fixed or variable frequency tone
  • Interface: 2-pin or 3-pin digital interface (VCC, GND, Signal)
  • Features: Active buzzer produces fixed tone on power; passive buzzer can generate different frequencies using PWM signals for melodies and alarms
  • Use in Lab: Used as audio feedback in IoT alert systems, security alarms, timer notifications, and smart home automation projects

clickHandlerArgs.title: Relay
clickHandlerArgs.description:
Relay

  • Type: Electromagnetic relay with optocoupler isolation
  • Channels: Single or multi-channel (1/2/4/8 channel variants)
  • Switching Capacity: 10A @ 250V AC / 10A @ 30V DC
  • Control Voltage: 3.3V or 5V logic-level trigger input
  • Features: LED indicators for relay status, normally open (NO) and normally closed (NC) contacts, screw terminal outputs for high-voltage connections
  • Use in Lab: Used for controlling high-power devices (lights, fans, motors, appliances) from low-power microcontrollers in home automation and industrial IoT projects

clickHandlerArgs.title: DHT11 Temperature & Humidity Sensor
clickHandlerArgs.description:
DHT11 Temperature & Humidity Sensor

  • Type: Digital temperature and humidity sensor with single-wire serial interface
  • Temperature Range: 0°C to 50°C with ±2°C accuracy
  • Humidity Range: 20% to 90% RH with ±5% RH accuracy
  • Operating Voltage: 3.3V to 5.5V DC, low power consumption
  • Interface: Single-bus digital signal output via 3-pin or 4-pin connector (VCC, DATA, GND)
  • Features: Built-in 8-bit microcontroller for calibrated digital output, 1 Hz sampling rate, compact form factor, no external components required
  • Use in Lab: Used in weather stations, greenhouse monitoring, HVAC control systems, indoor air quality tracking, and IoT-based environmental data logging projects

clickHandlerArgs.title: ESP8266 Wi-Fi Module
clickHandlerArgs.description:
ESP8266 Wi-Fi Module

  • Processor: Tensilica L106 32-bit RISC processor @ 80/160 MHz
  • Connectivity: IEEE 802.11 b/g/n Wi-Fi with WPA/WPA2 security
  • Memory: 64KB instruction RAM, 96KB data RAM, external Flash up to 16MB
  • GPIO: Up to 17 GPIO pins with PWM, I2C, SPI, UART, and 1 ADC (10-bit)
  • Operating Voltage: 3.3V with low-power deep sleep mode (~20 µA)
  • Features: Integrated TCP/IP stack, supports AT commands and Arduino IDE programming, OTA firmware updates
  • Use in Lab: Used for adding Wi-Fi connectivity to IoT projects, building web servers, connecting sensors to cloud platforms (ThingSpeak, Blynk, Firebase), and MQTT-based communication

clickHandlerArgs.title: ESP32 Microcontroller
clickHandlerArgs.description:
ESP32 Microcontroller

  • Processor: Xtensa dual-core 32-bit LX6 @ 240 MHz with ultra-low-power co-processor
  • Connectivity: Wi-Fi 802.11 b/g/n + Bluetooth 4.2 / BLE
  • Memory: 520KB SRAM, 448KB ROM, external Flash up to 16MB
  • GPIO: 34 programmable GPIOs with 18 ADC channels (12-bit), 2 DAC channels, 16 PWM, touch sensors
  • Interfaces: SPI, I2C, I2S, UART, CAN, Ethernet MAC, SD card interface
  • Features: Hall sensor, temperature sensor, hardware encryption (AES, SHA, RSA), deep sleep mode (~10 µA), over-the-air (OTA) updates
  • Use in Lab: Advanced IoT development platform for smart home systems, wearables, mesh networking, BLE beacons, and industrial IoT applications with dual wireless connectivity

clickHandlerArgs.title: Gas Sensor (MQ Series)
clickHandlerArgs.description:
Gas Sensor (MQ Series)

  • Type: Semiconductor metal oxide gas sensor (MQ-2 / MQ-5 / MQ-7 / MQ-135 variants)
  • Detection: LPG, methane, smoke, carbon monoxide, ammonia, benzene, alcohol, and air quality depending on model
  • Operating Voltage: 5V DC with onboard heater element
  • Output: Analog voltage output proportional to gas concentration + digital output via comparator with adjustable threshold (potentiometer)
  • Features: Fast response time, high sensitivity, long lifespan, simple 4-pin interface (VCC, GND, Analog Out, Digital Out)
  • Use in Lab: Used in gas leak detection systems, air quality monitoring, industrial safety alarms, and smart kitchen safety IoT projects

Gas Sensor (MQ Series)

  • Type: Semiconductor metal oxide gas sensor (MQ-2 / MQ-5 / MQ-7 / MQ-135 variants)
  • Detection: LPG, methane, smoke, carbon monoxide, ammonia, benzene, alcohol, and air quality depending on model
  • Operating Voltage: 5V DC with onboard heater element
  • Output: Analog voltage output proportional to gas concentration + digital output via comparator with adjustable threshold (potentiometer)
  • Features: Fast response time, high sensitivity, long lifespan, simple 4-pin interface (VCC, GND, Analog Out, Digital Out)
  • Use in Lab: Used in gas leak detection systems, air quality monitoring, industrial safety alarms, and smart kitchen safety IoT projects

clickHandlerArgs.title: Potentiometer
clickHandlerArgs.description:
Potentiometer

  • Type: Variable resistor (3-terminal) – rotary or linear taper
  • Resistance Range: Available in 1KΩ, 10KΩ, 50KΩ, 100KΩ values
  • Power Rating: Typically 0.1W to 0.5W
  • Interface: 3 pins – two end terminals connected across a resistive element and one wiper terminal providing variable voltage
  • Features: Provides proportional analog voltage output based on shaft rotation angle (0° to 270°), used as voltage divider
  • Use in Lab: Used for analog input experiments, volume/brightness control, servo motor angle control, ADC calibration, and as a user-input device in IoT prototyping

clickHandlerArgs.title: Raspberry Pi
clickHandlerArgs.description:
Raspberry Pi

  • Processor: Broadcom BCM2711 quad-core Cortex-A72 (ARM v8) 64-bit SoC @ 1.8 GHz (Raspberry Pi 4 Model B)
  • Memory: Available in 2GB, 4GB, or 8GB LPDDR4 RAM
  • Connectivity: Dual-band 2.4 GHz / 5 GHz Wi-Fi, Bluetooth 5.0, Gigabit Ethernet, 2× USB 3.0, 2× USB 2.0
  • GPIO: 40-pin GPIO header for interfacing sensors, motors, LEDs, and other peripherals
  • Display: Dual micro-HDMI outputs supporting up to 4K resolution
  • Features: Runs Linux-based OS (Raspberry Pi OS), supports Python, C/C++, Node.js, and Java; microSD card for storage
  • Use in Lab: Used for IoT prototyping, home automation projects, edge computing, server hosting, and integrating with cloud platforms like AWS IoT and Google Cloud IoT

clickHandlerArgs.title: Motor Driver (L298N)
clickHandlerArgs.description:
Motor Driver (L298N)

  • Type: Dual H-Bridge DC motor driver for bidirectional motor control
  • Voltage: Motor supply 5V to 35V, logic supply 5V
  • Current: Up to 2A per channel (L298N) with peak current of 3A
  • Channels: 2 DC motors or 1 stepper motor
  • Control Pins: Enable (PWM speed control), IN1/IN2 and IN3/IN4 (direction control)
  • Features: Onboard 5V regulator, heat sink for thermal management, LED indicators for motor status
  • Use in Lab: Used in robotics projects, smart vehicles, conveyor belt systems, and IoT-controlled motor applications for speed and direction control

hotSpots.text: Intel General Purpose FPGA Development Board

clickHandlerArgs.title: Intel General Purpose FPGA Development Board
clickHandlerArgs.description:
Intel General Purpose FPGA Development Board

  • FPGA: Intel (Altera) Cyclone series or MAX 10 FPGA with thousands of logic elements
  • Memory: Onboard SDRAM, Flash memory, and embedded memory blocks
  • I/O: Multiple GPIO headers, JTAG interface, USB-Blaster for programming
  • Interfaces: UART, SPI, I2C, ADC, VGA, 7-segment displays, switches, LEDs, push buttons
  • Features: Programmable using Verilog/VHDL with Intel Quartus Prime IDE, supports hardware description language debugging and simulation
  • Use in Lab: Used for digital logic design, custom hardware accelerators, real-time signal processing, and prototyping custom IoT processor architectures

clickHandlerArgs.title: Microsoft Azure Sphere MT3620
clickHandlerArgs.description:
Microsoft Azure Sphere MT3620

  • Processor: MediaTek MT3620 – ARM Cortex-A7 @ 500 MHz (application core) + 2× ARM Cortex-M4F (real-time cores)
  • Security: Microsoft Pluton security subsystem with hardware root of trust, certificate-based authentication, and secure boot
  • Connectivity: Dual-band Wi-Fi 802.11 a/b/g/n with built-in antenna
  • GPIO: Up to 72 GPIOs with ADC, UART, SPI, I2C, I2S, PWM
  • Features: Over-the-air (OTA) updates via Azure Sphere Security Service, 10+ year support lifecycle, end-to-end cloud security with Microsoft Azure IoT Hub
  • Use in Lab: Used for learning secure IoT development, building enterprise-grade connected devices with Azure cloud integration, and understanding hardware-level security in IoT ecosystems

clickHandlerArgs.title: Raspberry Pi Development Kit
clickHandlerArgs.description:
Raspberry Pi Development Kit

  • Contents: Raspberry Pi board, official power supply (5V/3A USB-C), microSD card with pre-installed Raspberry Pi OS, official case, micro-HDMI cable, and GPIO reference card
  • Accessories: Breadboard, jumper wires, resistors, LEDs, push buttons, and sensor starter pack
  • Camera: Raspberry Pi Camera Module v2 (8MP Sony IMX219 sensor) included for computer vision projects
  • Features: Complete out-of-the-box kit for beginners and advanced users, includes documentation and project guides
  • Use in Lab: All-in-one kit for rapid IoT prototyping, computer vision applications, home automation, weather stations, and teaching Python/Linux fundamentals on embedded hardware

clickHandlerArgs.title: Automatic Energy Meter Consumption Monitor
clickHandlerArgs.description:
Automatic Energy Meter Consumption Monitor

  • Type: IoT-based smart energy monitoring system (Mini Project)
  • Components: Current transformer (CT sensor), Arduino/ESP microcontroller, LCD display, Wi-Fi module, relay for load control
  • Measurements: Real-time voltage, current, power (watts), and energy consumption (kWh)
  • Communication: Wi-Fi-based data transmission to cloud dashboard for remote monitoring
  • Features: Automatic bill calculation, overload detection and alert, historical consumption data logging, energy-saving recommendations
  • Use in Lab: Demonstrates IoT application in smart grid and energy management – students learn sensor calibration, real-time data acquisition, cloud integration, and building practical smart home solutions

clickHandlerArgs.title: Mini Project – IoT Demonstration
clickHandlerArgs.description:
Mini Project – IoT Demonstration

  • Type: Student-built IoT demonstration project showcasing sensor integration
  • Components: Microcontroller (Arduino/ESP32), multiple sensors (temperature, humidity, gas, motion), actuators (relay, buzzer, LED), power supply module
  • Communication: Wi-Fi/Bluetooth connectivity with cloud platform integration (ThingSpeak/Blynk/Firebase)
  • Display: LCD/OLED screen for local data display
  • Features: Real-time sensor data monitoring, threshold-based alerts, mobile app control, data visualization dashboard
  • Use in Lab: Showcases end-to-end IoT system design from sensor interfacing to cloud data analytics – demonstrates the complete IoT development lifecycle including hardware assembly, firmware programming, and cloud deployment

clickHandlerArgs.title: 8085 Microprocessor Kit
clickHandlerArgs.description:
8085 Microprocessor Kit

  • Processor: Intel 8085A – 8-bit microprocessor @ 3 MHz / 5 MHz clock
  • Memory: 32KB ROM (monitor program) + 32KB RAM for user programs
  • I/O: 8255 PPI (Programmable Peripheral Interface), 8253 PIT (Programmable Interval Timer), 8259 PIC (Programmable Interrupt Controller), 8251 USART
  • Interface: Hex keypad for program entry, 6-digit seven-segment LED display for address/data, RS-232 serial port for PC communication
  • Instruction Set: 74 instructions – data transfer, arithmetic, logical, branching, and machine control operations
  • Features: Single-step execution, breakpoint debugging, memory examine/modify functions
  • Use in Lab: Foundational tool for learning microprocessor architecture, assembly language programming, hardware interrupt handling, and understanding CPU-memory-I/O bus organization

clickHandlerArgs.title: Mini Project 2 – Automatic Door Opener
clickHandlerArgs.description:
Mini Project 2 – Automatic Door Opener

  • Type: IoT-based contactless automatic door system (Student Mini Project)
  • Components: PIR motion sensor / ultrasonic sensor, servo motor / DC motor with driver, Arduino / ESP32 microcontroller, relay module, power supply
  • Operation: Detects human presence within range → triggers motor to open door → auto-closes after configurable delay
  • Communication: Optional Wi-Fi integration for remote control via mobile app and access logging
  • Features: Adjustable sensitivity and delay time, manual override switch, entry/exit counter, energy-efficient standby mode
  • Use in Lab: Practical demonstration of sensor-actuator integration, motor control algorithms, and IoT-based access control – applicable to smart buildings, hospitals, and retail environments

clickHandlerArgs.title: Auto Engine Control Unit (ECU)
clickHandlerArgs.description:
Auto Engine Control Unit (ECU)

  • Type: Automotive Electronic Control Unit – embedded system for engine management
  • Processor: Automotive-grade microcontroller (ARM Cortex-M / PowerPC based) with real-time processing capabilities
  • Sensors Interface: Crankshaft position, throttle position, mass air flow (MAF), oxygen sensor (lambda), coolant temperature, knock sensor inputs
  • Actuator Control: Fuel injectors, ignition coils, idle air control valve, EGR valve, variable valve timing
  • Communication: CAN bus (Controller Area Network), OBD-II diagnostic interface, LIN bus
  • Features: Real-time fuel injection timing, ignition advance mapping, emission control, fault code storage and diagnostic readout
  • Use in Lab: Demonstrates embedded systems in automotive IoT – students learn CAN bus communication, real-time sensor data processing, engine parameter tuning, and OBD-II diagnostics
